asset_name: Umbra Grab and Push State (4 frames, 10fps)
shared_properties: Umbra keeps the 32x48 px silhouette envelope, core palette metadata, and mist-fringe shape established by the idle base state.
The center-bottom pivot remains identical to the idle sheet so grab transitions do not move the collision capsule.
Hand silhouettes remain high-contrast against mirror edges and gold mechanical trim.
differences: Four frames show both hands extended toward a nearby interactable with a slight torso lean and bent rear knee.
The forward arm line stays readable against a 16 px mirror panel and a 32 px weight block edge.
A thin amber contact highlight appears only at the fingertip cluster to indicate active grab engagement.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the E-hold grab rule, 32 px interaction range, and object-manipulation play.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the metallic art deco material cues used around interactables.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra hand proportion and shoulder silhouette for contact poses.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the grab atlas during FloorLoading and use it when GrabState is active for UmbraActor.
runtime_notes: Keep the hands aligned to a 32 px interaction reach window.
Use one looping atlas rather than procedural arm offsets.
Preserve center-bottom pivot compatibility with idle and run states.

context.usage: Interaction state used while holding E near mirrors, prisms, or weight blocks within 32 px.

pose_notes: Front shoulder shifts 4 px forward on frame 2.
Rear heel remains planted within the 32 px body width.
Hand reach never exceeds the rightmost asset bound.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 128x48 px with 4 frames of 32x48 px and 0 px spacing.
The pose clearly communicates object manipulation against both mirror and weight silhouettes.
Runtime swap from idle to grab preserves the same center-bottom pivot.
